about.title: About
about.heading: Online Pixel Dungeon
about.p1: An online, real-time multiplayer remake inspired by <1>Shattered Pixel Dungeon</1> by Evan Debenham, itself based on the original <5>Pixel Dungeon</5> by Oleg Dolya.
about.p2: Art, audio and the title-screen design are from Shattered Pixel Dungeon, which is free software released under the GPLv3.

warrior.name: Warrior
warrior.desc: The Warrior endures extra damage with shielding granted by his broken seal. The seal can be moved between armors and transfers a single upgrade.

mage.name: Mage
mage.desc: The Mage is an arcane expert and carries a magical staff that's stronger than a wand. The staff can be imbued with any wand the Mage finds.

rogue.name: Rogue
rogue.desc: The Rogue can evade enemies and strike from invisibility using his cloak of shadows. He also detects secrets and traps from a greater distance.

huntress.name: Huntress
huntress.desc: The Huntress is a master of thrown weapons and has a magical bow with infinite arrows. She also travels through tall grass without trampling it.

app.descWelcome: A multiplayer roguelike dungeon crawler based on Shattered Pixel Dungeon. Play online for free in your browser, no download required.
app.titleSelect: Online Pixel Dungeon — Choose your class
app.descSelect: Select your hero class — Warrior, Mage, Rogue, or Archer — and descend into the dungeon.
app.descPlaying: Playing Online Pixel Dungeon — exploring floor {{depth}} of the dungeon.

chasm.title: Chasm
chasm.desc: Do you really want to jump into the chasm? A fall that far will be painful.
chasm.yes: Yes, I know what I'm doing

resurrect.title: Rise from the dead?
resurrect.desc: You are reborn on this floor with your gear intact. Half HP, debuffs cleared.
resurrect.descLost: You are reborn on this floor, but your gear was scattered where you fell. Half HP, debuffs cleared.

rankings.title: Rankings
rankings.name: Name
rankings.emptySub: Finished runs will appear here in a future update.
rankings.newsEmptySub: Check back later for updates and announcements.

progress.title: Progression
progress.desc: Based on maximum depth and hero level.

treasure.title: Treasure
treasure.desc: Based on gold collected and held item value.

explore.title: Exploration
explore.desc: Based on floors explored. Reduced by missing items or hidden doors.

bosses.title: Bosses
bosses.desc: Based on bosses defeated. Reduced by standing in avoidable attacks.

journal.title: Journal
journal.guideText: The dungeon is divided into 5 regions, each with a boss. Defeat bosses to progress deeper. Find the Amulet of Yendor on floor 26 and return to the surface to win.
journal.catalogEmpty: Item catalog tracking is not yet available in the online version.
journal.sewersStory: The sewers beneath the city are infested with rats, gnolls, and slimes. Goo, a conglomerate of vile substances, guards the way down.
journal.prisonStory: The abandoned prison is home to skeletons, thieves, and guards. Tengu, the enigmatic assassin, awaits in the depths.
journal.cavesStory: The caves are filled with bats, gnoll brutes, and shamans. DM-300, an ancient dwarven defense machine, guards the passage.
journal.cityStory: The dwarven city is ruled by the undead King of Dwarves. Monks, golems, and warlocks patrol the halls.
journal.hallsStory: The demon halls are the domain of Yog-Dzewa. Evil Eyes, scorpios, and ripper demons stalk the darkness.

challenges.title: Challenges
challenges.intro: Challenges make the game harder. They are set at character selection and cannot be changed mid-run.
challenges.stronger_bosses_desc:
Bosses are much tougher with this challenge!

_Goo:_ +20% health, heals faster in water, pumps up in 1 turn
_Tengu:_ +25% health, deadlier traps, more frequent abilities
_DM-300:_ +33% health, 3 pylons, stronger abilities
_Dwarf King:_ +50% health, stronger minions
_Yog-Dzewa:_ Two fists at once, +60% laser damage

desc.water: In case of burning step into the water to extinguish the fire.
desc.stairsUp: Stairs lead up to the upper depth.
desc.stairsDown: Stairs lead down to the lower depth.
desc.lockedDoor: This door is locked, you need a matching key to unlock it.
desc.lockedExit: Heavy bars block the stairs leading down.
desc.barricade: The wooden barricade is firmly set but has dried over the years. Might it burn?
desc.inactiveTrap: The trap has been triggered before and it's not dangerous anymore.
desc.statue: Someone wanted to adorn this place, but failed, obviously.
desc.alchemy: This pot is filled with magical water. Items can be mixed into the pot to create something new!
desc.crystalDoor: This locked door is made of magical crystal. You can see through, but you'll need a crystal key to open it.

trap.inactive: This trap is inactive and no longer dangerous.

desc.worn_dart: A small dart launcher. The mechanism looks worn, but it can still fire a dart at anything that steps on it.
desc.tengu_dart: A concealed dart trap. Tengu has rigged this part of the floor with poisoned darts.
desc.burning_trap: A trap that ignites a burst of flame when triggered.
desc.blazing_trap: A trap that unleashes a roaring blaze when triggered.

desc.regen: A magical remedy is causing wounds to close and flesh to knit. Every turn health will steadily regenerate until the healing effect expires.
desc.aqua_rejuv: A murky elixir that heals you whenever you stand in water.
desc.berserk: You are fueled by rage. Berserk grants greatly increased damage and shielding, but it fades quickly.
desc.endure: You are bracing for impact. Endure reduces incoming damage and banks the absorbed damage for your next strike.
desc.fury: You are angry, enemies won't like you when you're angry. A great rage burns within you, increasing the damage you deal with physical attacks by 50%.
desc.locked_floor: The floor is sealed by dark magic. You cannot regenerate health while the seal is active. Damage the boss to weaken the seal.

desc.rat: Marsupial rats are aggressive but rather weak denizens of the sewers. They have a nasty bite, but are only life threatening in large numbers.
desc.snake: These oversized serpents are capable of quickly slithering around blows, making them quite hard to hit. Magical attacks or surprise attacks are capable of catching them off-guard however.
desc.gnoll: Gnolls are hyena-like humanoids. They dwell in sewers and dungeons, venturing up to raid the surface from time to time. Gnoll scouts are regular members of their pack, they are not as strong as brutes and not as intelligent as shamans.
desc.swarm: The deadly swarm of flies buzzes angrily. Every non-magical attack will split it into two smaller but equally dangerous swarms.
desc.crab: These huge crabs are at the top of the food chain in the sewers. They are extremely fast and their thick carapace can withstand heavy blows.
desc.slime: Slimes are strange, slightly magical creatures with a rubbery outer body and a liquid core. The city sewers provide them with an ample supply of water and nutrients.
desc.albino_rat: This is a rare breed of marsupial rat, with pure white fur and jagged teeth.
desc.gnoll_exile: This grey-furred gnoll has a large mark on its shoulder, denoting it as an exile. Gnolls are usually exiled from their society for ignoring orders or breaking laws.
desc.hermit_crab: For one reason or another, this sewer crab has decided to wear the broken upper part of a barrel on its head! The extra weight has slown it down to regular speed, but has also granted it considerably more defence.
desc.caustic_slime: This slime seems to have been tainted by the dark magic emanating from below. It has lost its usual green color, and drips with caustic ooze.
desc.goo: Little is known about The Goo. It's quite possible that it is not even a creature, but rather a conglomerate of vile substances from the sewers that somehow gained basic intelligence.
desc.tengu: A famous and enigmatic assassin, named for the mask grafted to his face. He will try to use traps, deceptive magic, and precise attacks to eliminate the only thing stopping his escape: you.
desc.skeleton: Skeletons are composed of the bones of unlucky adventurers and inhabitants of the prison, animated by emanations of evil magic from the depths below.
desc.thief: Though these inmates roam free of their cells, this place is still their prison. Over time, this place has taken their minds as well as their freedom.
desc.guard: Once keepers of the prison, these guards have long since become no different than the inmates. They carry chains around their hip, possibly used to pull in enemies to close range.
desc.necromancer: These apprentice dark mages have flocked to the prison, as it is the perfect place to practise their evil craft. Killing the necromancer will also kill the skeleton it summons.
desc.bat: These brisk and tenacious inhabitants of the caves are much more dangerous than they seem. They replenish their health with each successful attack.
desc.brute: Brutes are the largest, strongest, and toughest of all gnolls. When mortally wounded, they go berserk, gaining temporary shielding and a large boost to damage.
desc.armored_brute: The most senior gnoll brutes often wear powerful armor to show their status. The armor makes these brutes much more resilient to physical damage.
desc.shaman: Gnoll shamans are intelligent gnolls who use battle spells to compensate for their lack of might.
desc.spinner: These greenish furry cave spiders try to avoid direct combat. Instead they prefer to wait in the distance while their victim struggles in their excreted cobweb.
desc.ghoul: As dwarven society slowly began to collapse, those who were weak or who resisted were bent to the king's will. When a ghoul is defeated, it will rise again after a few turns as long as another ghoul is nearby.
desc.warlock: These warlocks possess powerful disruptive magic, and are able to temporarily hinder the upgrade magic applied to your equipment.
desc.monk: These monks are fanatics who have devoted themselves to protecting their king through physical might. When focused, monks will parry the next physical attack used against them.
desc.senior: This monk has mastered the art of hand-to-hand combat, and is able to gain focus while moving much more quickly than regular monks.
desc.golem: Golems are an attempt to correct the previous flaws in dwarven machinery via the dwarves newfound use of magic. They can teleport between rooms.
desc.succubus: Succubi are shapeshifting demons that manipulate the minds of their prey. They may temporarily charm their enemy when they attack.
desc.eye: Evil Eyes are floating balls of pent up demonic energy. After building energy for a short time an Evil Eye will unleash a devastating beam called the deathgaze.
desc.scorpio: These huge arachnid-like demonic creatures avoid close combat, preferring to fire crippling serrated spikes from long distances.
desc.acidic_scorpio: These huge arachnid-like demonic creatures avoid close combat, preferring to fire crippling serrated spikes from long distances. This one appears to be dripping with corrosive ooze.
desc.ripper_demon: These horrific creatures are the result of the many dwarven corpses in this region being put to use by demonic forces. They are capable of leaping great distances to quickly reach targets.
desc.bandit: An elite thief, distinguished by their more opulent purple robe. In addition to stealing from you, crazed bandits will briefly blind, cripple, and poison you!
desc.wraith: A wraith is a vengeful spirit of a sinner, whose grave or tomb was disturbed. Being an ethereal entity, it is very hard to hit with a regular weapon.
desc.piranha: These carnivorous fish are bred specifically to protect flooded areas. Their bite is deadly, so it's best to avoid confronting them directly.
desc.phantom_piranha: Phantom piranhas are a rare breed of piranha with magical abilities and eerie translucent bodies. They are capable of teleporting as they are being attacked.
desc.mimic: Mimics are magical creatures which can take any shape they wish. In dungeons they almost always choose a shape of a treasure chest, in order to lure in unsuspecting adventurers.
desc.golden_mimic: Golden mimics are tougher mimics which try to attract the strongest adventurers. They have better loot, but are also much stronger than regular mimics.
desc.ebony_mimic: Ebony mimics are stealthy mimics with the ability to make themselves almost invisible. Their surprise bite is devastating.
desc.statue: You would think that it's just another one of this dungeon's inanimate statues, but its red glowing eyes give it away.
desc.armored_statue: It seems to be in great condition because of the armor it's wearing, it might be very hard to kill.
desc.bee: Despite their small size, golden bees tend to protect their home fiercely. This one is very mad, better keep your distance.

talent.title: Talents
talent.metamorphHint: Tap a talent to replace it
talent.blocker1: Reach level 7 to unlock more talents
talent.blocker2: Reach level 13 to unlock more talents
talent.blocker3: Reach level 21 to unlock more talents
talent.randomMsg: Choose how to assign remaining talent points for this tier.

subclass.title: Choose a Subclass
subclass.subtitle: You found Tengu's Mask. Choose your path.
subclass.confirm: Are you sure you want to choose this subclass?
subclass.skip: Decide later (press T to open talents)

berserker.short: A furious warrior who channels rage
berserker.full: A furious warrior who channels rage into devastating power. Unlocks rage mechanics and berserk transformation. The berserker grows stronger as they take damage, eventually entering a state of uncontrollable fury.

gladiator.short: Master of combat who builds momentum
gladiator.full: A master of combat who builds momentum through consecutive hits. Unlocks combo system with escalating finishers. Each hit builds combo power, unlocking devastating special moves at higher combo counts.

assassin.short: A deadly shadow who strikes from darkness
assassin.full: A deadly shadow who strikes from darkness with lethal precision. Bonuses to surprise attacks and stealth. Preparation time increases assassination damage dramatically.

freerunner.short: An agile rogue who moves freely
freerunner.full: An agile rogue who moves freely through the dungeon. Enhanced mobility, evasion, and stealth. Projectile momentum builds with movement speed.

battlemage.short: Mage who channels magic through staff
battlemage.full: A mage who channels magic through their staff in melee combat. Empowered strikes and staff charges. Each wand hit empowers the staff for devastating melee attacks.

warlock.short: Mage who drains life from enemies
warlock.full: A mage who drains life from enemies to sustain themselves. Soul eating, lifesteal, and necromancy. Kills restore health and raise minions to fight alongside you.

sniper.short: Huntress who picks off enemies from afar
sniper.full: A huntress who picks off enemies from afar with precision. Enhanced ranged attacks and vision. Shared enchantments and upgrades make the bow deadly at any range.

warden.short: Huntress who draws power from nature
warden.full: A huntress who draws power from nature and the dungeon's flora. Herbology, barkskin, and nature magic. Grass and dew provide healing, shielding, and armor.

ability.title: Choose an Armor Ability
ability.subtitle: You found the King's Crown. Imbue your armor with a powerful ability.
ability.confirm: Are you sure you want to choose this ability?
ability.skip: Decide later (press T to open talents)

heroic_leap.name: Heroic Leap
heroic_leap.desc: Leap and slam, damaging and stunning

death_mark.name: Death Mark
death_mark.desc: Mark an enemy for bonus damage

shadow_clone.name: Shadow Clone
shadow_clone.desc: Create a phantom copy of yourself

warp_beacon.name: Warp Beacon
warp_beacon.desc: Place and recall to a beacon

spirit_hawk.name: Spirit Hawk
spirit_hawk.desc: Summon a spirit hawk to scout

ghostGear.title: Ghost Equipment
ghostGear.selectWeapon: Choose a weapon for the ghost

alchemy.title: Alchemy
alchemy.hint: Combine ingredients to create something new!
alchemy.noRecipe: These ingredients don't combine into anything

potions.title: Intro and Potions
potions.body:
Welcome to Practical Applications of Alchemy! This book is a reference of recipes to craft items at an alchemy pot.

We'll start with the most iconic recipe: Potions!

Use three seeds to brew a random potion! Every seed has a potion counterpart, use multiple of the same seed to increase the chance that the potion relates to the seeds you used.

stones.title: Creating Runestones
stones.body: Mixing a scroll into an alchemy pot will imbue its magic into two rocks within the pot. This creates runestones and identifies the scroll!

energy_food.title: Energy and Food
energy_food.body:
Many recipes require crystal alchemical energy, which is made by deconstructing consumable items in an alchemy pot. Energizing potions or scrolls also identifies them.

Some recipes that require energy are magical, but these ones resemble traditional cooking.

exotic_potions.title: Exotic Potions
exotic_potions.body: Potions can be mixed with energy to create exotic potions. They have more powerful effects, but are often useful in different ways.

exotic_scrolls.title: Exotic Scrolls
exotic_scrolls.body: Exotic scrolls can be made with some energy and a scroll. They're a bit stronger and more expensive than exotic potions.

bombs.title: Enhanced Bombs
bombs.body: A standard black powder bomb can be mixed with a specific item to create an enhanced bomb.

weapons.title: Enhancing Weapons
weapons.body:
Some of the lighter or more magical weapons can be used in alchemy!

Each thrown weapon set produces enough liquid metal to fully repair another set of the same level and tier. Note that alchemizing a set will destroy the whole set, even if it isn't all present!

One wand will produce enough arcane resin to upgrade two wands of the same level, but no higher than +3.

brews_elixirs.title: Brews and Elixirs
brews_elixirs.body: Brews and elixirs are advanced potions with a variety of effects, usually with a single use.

spells.title: Spells
spells.body: Spells are contained in crystals and provide a variety of effects, usually with multiple uses.

guide.title: Getting Started
guide.body1: Pick a class from the hero select screen, optionally set a difficulty and name, then enter the dungeon.
guide.body2: Click a tile to walk there. Click an adjacent enemy to attack it.
guide.body3: Your goal is to descend as deep as you can — find the stairs down on each floor.
guide.combat1: Equip weapons and armor from your inventory; ranged weapons enter a targeting mode — click a tile to fire.
guide.combat2: Drink potions and use items from the toolbar at the bottom of the screen.
guide.combat3: Watch your health: it regenerates slowly over time, and a low-health warning will sound.
guide.classes1: Warrior — sturdy melee fighter, starts with a shortsword and cloth armor.
guide.classes2: Mage — wields a magic staff with limited charges.
guide.classes3: Rogue — agile, starts with a dagger and cloak.
guide.classes4: Archer — ranged specialist with a spirit bow.
guide.multiplayer1: The dungeon is shared in real time over WebSocket — other players appear on your floor.
guide.multiplayer2: If you fall, you can start a new run or return to the main menu from the game-over screen.

changelog.title: v0.7.0
changelog.changes0: In-place respawn on Easy and Medium difficulty — your hero is reborn at the floor's stairs with 50% HP, debuffs cleared and a 3-turn spawn-protection window. Max 3 resurrections per run; boss-floor deaths are final (no respawn on floors 5, 10, 15, ...).
changelog.changes1: Easy keeps your full gear on respawn; Medium keeps just your equipped weapon and armor; Hard has no respawn and scatters everything. UI difficulty labels renamed: NORMAL → MEDIUM, HARD → NORMAL — only the labels moved, not the difficulty progression or mob-AI aggression tiers.
changelog.changes2: Score penalties for respawns: each resurrection halves your score (3 respawns → 12.5%); witnessing a teammate's resurrection shaves 25% per use, floored at 10%.
changelog.changes3: Resurrect dialog and HUD badge show respawns remaining; the death screen reflects whether your gear was scattered where you fell.
changelog.changes4: Fixed React StrictMode double-connect leaving heroes stuck as AFK ghosts when a stale first socket's disconnect arrived after the second socket had already rebound for the same session.
changelog.changes5: Trap visibility fix: Worn Dart, Poison Dart, Rockfall, Disintegration and Grim traps are now always visible (matching SPD's canBeHidden=false) instead of sometimes spawning hidden.
changelog.changes6: Removed the obsolete entrance-room passive heal; the well_fed buff now drives the 3x regen bonus on its own. Mob door-open sounds now broadcast explicitly, and other players' HP bars are always visible (2px tall, repositioned above the sprite).
changelog.v0620: When your connection drops, your hero now becomes an invisible, un-targetable "(AFK)" ghost instead of freezing in place; if you don't reconnect in time it dies through the normal death path (loot drop, grave) instead of vanishing.
changelog.v0621: Reconnecting or refreshing the page now resumes your existing hero — position, HP and gear intact — instead of spawning a new one.
changelog.v0622: Swarm splitting now conserves HP across clones and spawns them next to the Swarm itself, matching the original game instead of appearing behind or beside the player.
changelog.v0623: Mirror Image clones (Scroll of Mirror Image, Multiplicity glyph, Cursed Wand of Summon Monsters) now fight using the hero's actual weapon and ring stats instead of flat approximations.
changelog.v0624: Sewers: six previously-empty standard rooms (Minefield, Plants, Aquarium, Fissure, Grassy Grave, Study) now have their real terrain, loot and mobs; Sentry, Magic Well and Sacrifice Fire special rooms are functional again.
changelog.v0625: New in-game Alchemy Guide available from the alchemy pot's Guide button; ghost reward window and loot indicator visuals reworked to match the original.
changelog.v0626: Alchemy pot workspace restyled to match the original look (red ingredient slots, correct hint text, matching button colors); tombs and chests now show distinct names and sounds.
changelog.v0610: Hit reactions now use SPD's two-tier warning sound — a sharper critical alert layers on top of the low-health warning, based on how much damage just landed and how little HP remains.
changelog.v0611: Fixed boss mobs (like Goo) sometimes getting stuck at their spawn tile when their arena overlapped the level's entrance or secret room.
changelog.v0612: Mobs now track whether they can actually see their target and chase more reliably through doors, instead of losing the trail the instant line of sight briefly breaks.
changelog.v0600: Wandmaker quest on Prison depths 6-9: Corpse Dust, Rotberry and Ceremonial Candle variants, each granting a wand reward.
changelog.v0601: Library special rooms: bookshelf wall stitching plus crystal doors and barricades rendered correctly in the wall atlas.
changelog.v0602: Fixed a wall-atlas regression that broke ordinary rooms' straight side walls with black gaps.
changelog.v0603: Server stability: isolated per-game exceptions in the global game loop and fixed a connection-list mutation race, so one game crashing no longer freezes broadcasting for every other game on the server.
changelog.v0604: Wooden special-room floors (Library, Storage, Treasury) now play the original SPD 'sturdy' step sound instead of the generic footstep.
changelog.v0500: New full-screen Alchemy scene: walk into the alchemy pot to open a redesigned brewing workspace with a water background and live bubble particles.
changelog.v0501: Ingredient picker now follows Shattered Pixel Dungeon recipe rules — only valid reagents (identified/uncursed wands, uncursed trinkets, potions, scrolls, seeds and more) can be selected.
changelog.v0502: Alchemy pots on the map now bubble with animated particles.
changelog.v0503: Rendering fix: wall tops bordering visible floor no longer render as black gaps.
changelog.v00x1: Four playable classes: Warrior, Mage, Rogue, Archer.
changelog.v00x2: Canvas-rendered dungeon with mobs, items, inventory and combat.
changelog.v0100: New animated main menu: scrolling parallax background, banner with pulsing glow, and two flickering torches — ported from the original Shattered Pixel Dungeon title screen.
changelog.v0101: Added Settings (audio + display), Changes, Guide, About, Rankings and News screens.
changelog.v0102: Audio now has independent music and SFX volume controls plus a master mute.
changelog.v0103: Display option to toggle background animations.
changelog.v0200: SPD-style critical hits: surprise attacks, damage floors, Fury and weapon enchants.
changelog.v0201: Misses now show visual and audio feedback.
changelog.v0202: 8-directional movement with diagonal walking; smoother keyboard input.
changelog.v0203: Line-of-sight rewritten with the original recursive shadowcasting.
changelog.v0204: Visible traps with proper terrain rendering.
changelog.v0205: Mobile toolbar: quick-bag, radial menu and slot swapping; fixed mobile travel and ranged attacks.
changelog.v0206: Custom cursor across landing and hero screens; camera keeps the player centered at high zoom.
changelog.v0207: Ported the original inventory and UI elements; fixed item inspect, throwing and labels.
changelog.v0208: Reworked armor and damage flow; fixed mob attack timing and global passive healing.
changelog.v0209: Entrance room is now a healing room.
changelog.v0210: SEO: full meta tags, robots.txt and sitemap.
changelog.v0300: Full SPD-faithful level generation for all 5 dungeon regions: Sewers, Prison, Caves, City and Halls.
changelog.v0301: New floor 5/10/15/20/25 boss fights: Goo, Tengu, DM-300, Dwarf King and Yog-Dzewa, each with their own AI and arena.
changelog.v0302: 24 new mob types added across the new regions.
changelog.v0303: Hunger system: characters start starving at 450 and food items reduce hunger.
changelog.v0304: All 12 base potions, 12 base scrolls and food items added.
changelog.v0305: New combat sound effects for blasts, lightning, rays, scrolls and locks.
changelog.v0310: Shop rooms: buy and sell items with gold.
changelog.v0311: Gold pickup and item value/identification data for the full item set.
changelog.v0313: Quickslot bag UI improvements and wall-fog rendering fixes.
changelog.v0400: Full Warrior talent tree: T1-T3 talents, Berserk and Combo mechanics, Gladiator finisher moves.
changelog.v0402: Expanded melee weapon roster and weapon enchantments/curses.
changelog.v0403: Admin item browser (press U) to spawn any item for testing.
changelog.v0404: Combat rework: damage multipliers/bonuses, guaranteed hits, and enchant-driven procs.
changelog.v0410: Add per-scroll particle emitters mirroring SPD's Speck types.
changelog.v0412: Rewrite StatusPane to large-layout (PC) with health/shield/EXP bars.
changelog.v0420: Added Tengu's Mask and King's Crown items with inventory serialization and sprite fixes.
changelog.v0421: Fixed Tengu arena layout level exiting and boss tracking mechanics.
changelog.v0422: Resolved issues with integer conversion of entity health during state updates.
changelog.v0440: Scroll of Recharging changed from instant full refill to regen-speed multiplier (matches SPD).
changelog.v0441: Scroll of Teleportation prefers SpecialRoom interiors; secret doors auto-discovered at destination.
changelog.v0442: Scroll of Identify/Remove Curse/Transmutation: dialog always opens; unidentified scrolls consumed on read (SPD match).
changelog.v0443: Scroll of Rage no longer amoks allies (matches SPD).
changelog.v0444: Scroll of Magic Mapping only marks discoverable tiles; secret discovery restricted to FOV.
changelog.v0445: Blindness and magic immunity block scroll reading (SPD match).
changelog.v0446: Scroll generation uses SPD probability weights for balanced loot distribution.
changelog.v0447: Degrade buff removed on Upgrade/Remove Curse scroll use (SPD match).
changelog.v0448: Fixed ReferenceError crash on surprise attacks (snake vs mirror image).
changelog.v0449: Admin auto-identifies picked up potions/scrolls for easier testing.
changelog.v0450: Fixed side door open/closed state not syncing between clients.
changelog.v0460: New playable class: Huntress with SPD-accurate Spirit Bow (infinite ammo, level-scaling, auto-quickslot).
changelog.v0461: All 12 SPD rings: Force, Elements, Wealth — full ring generation, identification and equipment.
changelog.v0462: SPD-accurate scroll behaviors: Teleportation prefers secret rooms, Identify/Remove Curse/Transmutation always opens dialog.
changelog.v0463: Invisibility potion with gradual fade (0.4 alpha over 0.4s matching SPD) and mob last-known-position AI.
changelog.v0464: Magic missile glow trail, auto-target double-click, LOS-free wand zaps via ballistica.
changelog.v0465: Wand of Lightning VFX/SFX, electricity blob lifetime cap, Goo death drops.
changelog.v0466: Per-weapon melee hit sounds, boss health bars, bag tabs on equip row (SPD layout).
changelog.v0467: Bug fixes: player vanishing, sub-bag scatter on death, surprise attack crash, hunger starvation disabled, SPD-faithful Sewers lore.
changelog.v04110: Interactive attack demo canvas (bump + ranged) in the onboarding tutorial.
changelog.v04111: Flying item pickup animation: items fly to the inventory button on pickup.
changelog.v04112: Toolbar quickslots: waterskin volume (N/20), dynamic font sizing for counts, hover tooltips with keybind hints.
changelog.v04113: Inventory pane: bag capacity captions (N/M) on tabs, zoom compensation, just-identified glow, equipped badge (E).
changelog.v04114: Enhanced (blue) / warning (orange) upgrade level tints in inventory, pure level-color module.
changelog.v04115: Item inspect via long-press/right-click in selector mode, inline selector prompt, Tab/[ ] bag cycling.
changelog.v04116: Desktop hover tooltips with keybind hints for quickslot items.

movement.title: Movement
movement.body: Use these keys to move your hero:

examine.title: Examine & Reveal
examine.body: Press E to enter examine mode, then click a tile or mob to inspect it.
examine.sub: Press E again to search the area and reveal hidden passages.

attack.title: Combat
attack.body: Walk into adjacent enemies to attack them.
attack.sub: Select a ranged weapon and tap a target to fire.

objectives.title: Your Goal
objectives.body: Defeat monsters, find the stairs down, and descend deeper.
